title: herniaria hirsuta var. cinerea
intro: Herniaria hirsuta var. cinerea, also known as cinereous rampion or cinereous hairy rampion, is a variety of the Herniaria hirsuta plant species within the carnation family. It is a perennial herb with a history of medicinal use, valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including diarrhea, dysentery, and wounds, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herniaria hirsuta var. cinerea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health benefits.
